message.content:
다음 PDF를 읽고, OnRobot Screwdriver를 TMflow에서 사용하기 위한 핵심 정보를 추출해줘. 한글로 정리해서 반환해.

파일: C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot\User_Manual_For_Techman_Robots_Quick_Changer_Screwdriver_v6.5.0_EN.pdf

추출할 내용:
1. Quick Changer 설정 (타입, 장착 방법)
2. Screwdriver TMflow 컴포넌트 노드 목록 및 각 노드의 파라미터 상세 (CONFIG, PICKUP, TIGHTEN, LOOSEN, MOVE, PREMOUNT, SELFTAPPING 등)
3. 노드별 입력/출력 변수 목록
4. XML-RPC API 함수 목록 및 파라미터 (Compute Box 원격 제어)
5. TCP/CoG 설정값 (스크루드라이버 장착 시)
6. Compute Box 설정 (네트워크, I/O)
7. 에러코드 목록 및 의미
8. 듀얼 스크루드라이버(D2SD) 관련 정보

모든 파라미터의 타입, 범위, 기본값이 있으면 함께 포함해.

   986→5.4.3.1. ScrewDriver_OR_SD_110_TIGHTEN
   987→Tightens a picked up screw. Extends the shank while rotating the bit head clockwise. The
   988→Screwdriver detects the start of the operation when the Z force is applied on the shank. The
   989→operation is done when the shank has moved the distance of the screw length and the screw
   990→torque is exceeded. Followed by a 10 mm retraction of the shank.

   997→35.0 [mm]. The default value is 5 mm.

  1422→5.4.3.5. ScrewDriver_OR_SD_110_SELFTAPPING
  1423→Screws in a self-tapping screw to a pre-drilled hole. Extends the shank while rotating the bit
  1424→head clockwise. The Screwdriver detects the start of the operation when the Z force is
  1425→applied on the shank. The operation is done when the shank has moved the distance of the
  1426→screw length and the screw torque is exceeded. Followed by a 10 mm retraction of the shank.
  1427→NOTE:
  1428→For self-tapping screws, a hole is needed according to the screw manufacturer,
  1429→as the Screwdriver can only create a thread in the hole but not the hole itself.
  1430→This component has three parameters:

  1432→ZForceN: The amount of force applied on the shank – 18.0 – 30.0 N. The default value is
  1433→18 N.

  1435→ScrewLengthMM: The length of the screw, the distance the screw needs to go in – 0.1 -
  1436→35.0 mm. The default value is 5 mm.
  1437→When using pointing screws, measure the screw length from the head of the
  1438→screw to the bottom where the pointing part begins.
  1439→•
  1440→TargetTorqueN: The torque value that the Screwdriver will try to reach at the end of the
  1441→process execution – 0.15 - 5.0 N. The default value is: 0.5 N.

  1825→6. Additional Software Options
  1826→6.1. Compute Box/Eye Box
  1827→6.1.1. Web Client
  1828→To access the Web Client on your computer first the Ethernet interface needs to be set up to
  1829→have a proper communication between your computer and the Compute Box. It is
  1830→recommended to use the factory default DIP switch settings (DIP 3 On and DIP 4 Off) (for
  1831→further details see section Ethernet Interface Setup).
  1832→Then do the following steps:
  1833→•
  1834→Connect the Compute Box to your computer with the supplied UTP cable.
  1835→•
  1836→Power the Compute Box with the supplied power supply.
  1837→•
  1838→Wait one minute for the Compute Box LED to turn from blue to green.
  1839→•
  1840→To find the IP address of the Compute Box, open the OnRobot Discovery Tool.
  1841→•
  1842→Open a web browser on your computer and type in the IP address of the Compute Box
  1843→(factory default is 192.168.1.1).
  1844→The Sign-in page opens:
  1845→The factory default administrator login is:
  1846→Username: admin
  1847→Password: OnRobot
  1848→For the first login a new password needs to be entered: (password must be at least 8
  1849→characters long)

  1935→Pre-mount screw
  1936→NOTE:
  1937→Pre-mount can only be used with machine screws and not with self-tapping
  1938→screws.
  1939→•
  1940→Screwing length: Input the target screwing length.
  1941→•
  1942→Torque limit: Input the target tightening torque. The torque limit defines the maximum
  1943→torque the Screwdriver can reach during the pre-mounting action.
  1944→•
  1945→Run: Press to execute the Screwdriver command.
  1946→•
  1947→Stop: Press to stop the Screwdriver command that is executed at that time.
  1948→Tighten / Loosen screw
  1949→•
  1950→Self-tapping: When selected, the Screwdriver will use 85% of the final torque and up to 3
  1951→Nm during the self-tapping phase. Then it will apply the set tightening torque for the final
  1952→tightening phase.
  1953→NOTE:
  1954→For self-tapping screws, a hole is needed according to the screw
  1955→manufacturer, as the Screwdriver can only create a thread in the hole but
  1956→not the hole itself.
  1957→•
  1958→Screw type: The screw sizes can be selected from the list. This selection will autofill the
  1959→standard torque value for that particular screw type.
  1960→ADDITIONAL SOFTWARE OPTIONS
  1961→62
  1962→
  1963→
  1964→=== PAGE 63 ===
  1965→•
  1966→Tightening Torque: Input the set tightening torque.
  1967→NOTE:
  1968→Use a chamfer for better reliability when making the screw hole.
  1969→•
  1970→Screwing length = A-B-C: The resulting screwing length is shown here. This is the amount
  1971→of the screw that will be screwed into/unscrewed from the thread. To set the value use
  1972→input fields below and the resulting amount will be A (screw length) - B (Washer thickness)
  1973→- C (Chamfer deepness).
  1974→The image shows 3 different lengths:
  1975→•
  1976→A = This is the screw length, distance from the bottom to the head of the screw.
  1977→When using pointing screws, measure the screw length from the head of
  1978→the screw to the bottom where the pointing part begins.
  1979→•
  1980→B = This is the washer thickness (or piece in between screw and thread), if no piece is
  1981→used, set it as 0. This amount will be subtracted from the Screw length to provide the
  1982→resulting Screwing length.
  1983→•
  1984→C = This is the chamfer deepness. This amount will be subtracted from the Screw
  1985→length to provide the resulting Screwing length.
  1986→•
  1987→Tighten: When this command is executed, the Screwdriver will move and rotate the
  1988→Screw-bit System until it reaches the thread. Then, it will start to screw in the screw until
  1989→90% of the Screwing length is inside the thread. During the rest 10% of the Screwing
  1990→length, the Screwdriver will tighten the screw applying the Target torque. If Self-tapping is
  1991→selected, the Screwdriver will behave as described above in the Self-tapping description.
  1992→•
  1993→Loosen: When this command is executed, the Screwdriver will move and rotate the bit to
  1994→align it with the screw head. Then, it will loosen and unscrew the screw until it is out the
  1995→thread (specified screwing length). Afterwards, it will hide the Screw-bit System inside the
  1996→housing.
  1997→•
  1998→Stop: This will stop the Screwdriver command that is executed at that time.
  1999→Move Shank
  2000→•

2001→Shank position: Changes the position of the shank immediately.

  2051→6.1.3. Web Client: Configuration Menu
  2052→Network settings:
  2053→The MAC address is a world-wide unique identifier that is fixed for the device.
  2054→The Network mode drop-down menu can be used to decide if the Compute Box will have a
  2055→static or a dynamic IP address:
  2056→ADDITIONAL SOFTWARE OPTIONS
  2057→65
  2058→
  2059→
  2060→=== PAGE 66 ===
  2061→•
  2062→If it is set to Dynamic IP, the Compute Box expects an IP address from a DHCP server. If
  2063→the network that the device is connected to has no DHCP server, the Compute Box will not
  2064→obtain an IP address and its LED is lighting in blue.
  2065→•
  2066→If it is set to Static IP, then a fixed IP address and subnet mask must be set.
  2067→•
  2068→If it is set to Default Static IP, the fixed IP revert to the factory default and cannot be
  2069→changed.
  2070→After all parameters are set, click on the Save button to store the new values permanently.
  2071→Wait 1 minute and reconnect to the device using the new settings.
  2072→Compute Box / Eye Box settings:
  2073→In case, more than one Compute Box is used within the same network, for identification
  2074→purpose any user specific name can be entered to the Display name.
  2075→If the Clock delay field shows a difference, click Synchronize clock to synchronize the
  2076→Compute Box's time with your computer.
  2077→EtherNet/IP scanner settings:
  2078→NOTE:
  2079→This is a special option of the EtherNet/IP connection for some robots.
  2080→In case when the robot is the Adapter and the Compute Box needs to be the Scanner the
  2081→following addition information is required for the communication:
  2082→•
  2083→IP address to connect to - the robot IP address
  2084→•
  2085→Origin-to-target instance id - refer to the robot's EtherNet/IP manual (Scanner mode)
  2086→•
  2087→Target-to-origin instance id - refer to the robot's EtherNet/IP manual (Scanner mode)
  2088→•
  2089→Configuration instance id - refer to the robot's EtherNet/IP manual (Scanner mode)
  2090→•
  2091→Requested packet interval (ms) - RPI value in ms (minimum 4)
  2092→Check the checkbox and the Compute Box will try to automatically connect to the robot (via
  2093→the given IP address).
  2094→For information on the Robot settings and the WebLytics settings, see the WebLytics Manual.

  2765→There are three potential outcomes when testing a self-tapping screw:
  2766→1.
  2767→The screw goes all the way in and is tightened with the set target torque. This is
  2768→successful operation.
  2769→2. The screw breaks while screwing in and the Screwdriver returns a result code / runtime
  2770→error: 10 - “Torque dropped unexpectedly”. This means that the screw cannot handle such
  2771→high torque on a material that hard.
  2772→3. The Screwdriver stops halfway through and returns a result code / runtime error: 4 -
  2773→“Torque exceeded prematurely”. This means that a higher torque is needed to go through
  2774→that material with that screw. A solution could be to set a higher tightening torque.
  2775→HARDWARE SPECIFICATION

  2784→Screw-bit System
  2785→This system will highly increase the efficacy of the screws to be picked up, aligned with the
  2786→bit, moved around with the Screwdriver and screwed in/out. Therefore, it is highly
  2787→recommended to set up the Screw-bit System correctly to keep a high success rate.
  2788→Example of the Screw-bit System for an ISO 14579, M2 screw.

  2799→The following sections explain the different components of the Screw-bit System and how to
  2800→set it up correctly.
  2801→Screws
  2802→The first step is to know what type of screw is going to be used. The screw type will define
  2803→what type of screw fix (in any), screw carrier, bit, and bit holder shall be used.
  2804→NOTE:
  2805→Use a chamfer for better reliability when making the screw hole.
  2806→The recommended screw types for the Screwdriver are the ones that have the properties
  2807→mentioned previously in the Supported Screws tables.
  2808→Screw Fix and Screw Carrier
  2809→Select the right screw fix and screw carrier depending on the screw type and the size to
  2810→maximize the efficacy of the Screw-bit System based on the table in section:

  2895→Bit Holder
  2896→Select the right bit holder depending on the screw type and size to maximize the efficacy of
  2897→the Screw-bit System based on the table in section:
  2898→•
  2899→Metric Screws
  2900→•
  2901→US Standard Screws
  2902→•
  2903→Self-tapping Screws for Aluminium
  2904→•
  2905→Self-tapping Screws for Plastic
  2906→The bit holder generates a magnetic force that will keep the screw attached and aligned to
  2907→the bit.
  2908→There are two types of bit holders:
  2909→HARDWARE SPECIFICATION
  2910→86
  2911→
  2912→
  2913→=== PAGE 87 ===
  2914→•
  2915→Bit Holder A: Generates a higher magnetic force. It is commonly used for the bigger and
  2916→heavier screws.
  2917→•
  2918→Bit Holder B: Generates a lower magnetic force. It is commonly used for the smaller and
  2919→lighter screws.
  2920→WARNING:
  2921→If Bit Holder A is used for smaller and lighter screws instead of Bit Holder B, the
  2922→screws can jump from the Screw Feeder to the Screwdriver because of the
  2923→higher magnetic force.
  2924→Bit Extenders 50 and 100 mm
  2925→The bit extenders are a long version of the previously described bit holders. Bit extenders are
  2926→useful to reach narrow spaces.
  2927→The bit extenders have a lock nut to tighten against the screw carrier to ensure that the screw
  2928→carrier does not move out of position over time.
  2929→When the bit extenders are mounted on the Screwdriver, the maximum total radial runout can
  2930→be up to 0.5 mm (measured below the thread as shown in the following picture).
  2931→The bit extenders need to be purchased separately by contacting your vendor where the
  2932→Screwdriver was purchased.
  2933→•
  2934→Bit extender type A 50 mm - PN 109301
  2935→•
  2936→Bit extender type B 50 mm - PN 109289
  2937→•
  2938→Bit extender type A 100 mm - PN 109290
  2939→•
  2940→Bit extender type B 100 mm - PN 109298
  2941→For more information on the mechanical dimensions, go to the 7.2.2.1. Mechanical Drawings

  3031→To attach the Screw-bit System to the Screwdriver, follow the instructions below.
  3032→1.
  3033→Move the shank to the highest possible
  3034→value by using the user interface in the
  3035→robot or in the Web Client.
  3036→2. Detach the Screwdriver from the Quick
  3037→Changer.

  3043→3. Remove the lid.
  3044→4. Place the hex shape of the bit holder inside
  3045→of the end of the Screwdriver's shank. The
  3046→system will be attached to the Screwdriver
  3047→by a magnetic force.

  3051→Detaching the Screw-bit System from the Screwdriver
  3052→To remove the Screw-bit System from the Screwdriver's shank, follow the instructions below.

  3054→Move the shank all the way out to the
  3055→highest possible value by operating the
  3056→user interface in the robot or in the Web
  3057→Client.
  3058→2. Use the provided key to grab the bit holder.
  3059→While holding the key, move the shank
  3060→inwards (to a lower value) by operating the
  3061→user interface in the robot or in the Web
  3062→Client.

  3323→Screwdriver Position to Execute Commands
  3324→To successfully execute the Screwdriver commands, it is fundamental to position the
  3325→Screwdriver correctly. This is achieved if the following two conditions are met:
  3326→1.
  3327→The Screw-bit System must be perfectly aligned to
  3328→the screw or thread.
  3329→2. The distance between the Screwdriver's bottom part
  3330→and the surface where the action takes place must
  3331→be within the range of 0-8 mm [0-0.31 inches].
  3332→To successfully execute the Screwdriver commands, it is fundamental to operate the
  3333→Screwdriver downwards or maximum sidewards. The Screwdriver should not be operated
  3334→upwards or with an angle higher than 90° orientate with respect to the ground, since this will
  3335→trigger the protective feature.
  3336→HARDWARE SPECIFICATION
  3337→102
  3338→

  3364→Torque Angle Curve and Torque Gradient
  3365→The torque gradient shows how the torque is applied in the last phase of the Tightening
  3366→screw command. This could be used as an indicator to detect if a Tightening command is
  3367→performed correctly.
  3368→NOTE:
  3369→When using self-tapping screws, if the tapping torque is very close to the target
  3370→torque, the torque gradient might provide an invalid value.
  3371→For instance, the torque gradient could be different if:
  3372→•
  3373→The hole thread is not long enough
  3374→•
  3375→The hole thread is different from the screw thread
  3376→•
  3377→The hole thread is not clean (for instance by deburrs from CNC machining)
  3378→•
  3379→The friction between the screw thread and the hole thread is too low or too high
  3380→•
  3381→The friction between the screw head and the tighten part is too low or too high
  3382→A torque gradient variable is made available to be checked in the robot program.
  3383→The graph below shows a normal Torque/Angle curve. In this case has been made with an M4
  3384→screw and 2.4 Nm as target torque.

  3689→8. Maintenance
  3690→WARNING:
  3691→An overall inspection of OnRobot's end-of-arm tooling and accessories must be
  3692→performed regularly and at least once every 6 months. This inspection must
  3693→include but is not limited to check for defective material and clean gripping
  3694→surfaces.
  3695→Use original spare parts, and original service instructions for OnRobot's end-of-
  3696→arm tooling, accessories and the robot. Failure to comply with this precaution
  3697→can cause unexpected risks, resulting in severe injury.
  3698→If you have questions regarding spare parts and repair, please visit our website
  3699→www.onrobot.com to contact us.
  3700→8.1. Screwdriver
  3701→Screw Fix and Bit
  3702→NOTE:
  3703→The screw fixes and bits are wear and tear parts, therefore they must be
  3704→inspected regularly.
  3705→These parts must be inspected minimum once a week or after 60.000 cycles.
  3706→If a screw fix is damaged due to misuse, it must be replaced.
  3707→If the Pick Screw process is failing, it might be an indicator that the screw fix is worn or has
  3708→been damaged.
  3709→WARNING:
  3710→The Screw-bit System should be visually inspected every time the robot is
  3711→reset or once per week, depending on application and use.
  3712→WARNING:
  3713→Check the inside of the screw bit holder regularly for debris. Make sure that the
  3714→magnetic interface is clear from metal shavings and dirt.
  3715→WARNING:
  3716→Always detach the Screwdriver from the Quick Changer to perform cleaning or
  3717→maintenance.
  3718→MAINTENANCE
  3719→113
  3720→
  3721→
  3722→=== PAGE 114 ===
  3723→O-Ring Replacement
  3724→NOTE:
  3725→The O-Rigs are wear and tear parts, therefore they must be inspected regularly.
  3726→This part must be inspected minimum once a month. To inspect the O-Ring,
  3727→attach a bit holder to the shank and check if it is straight and kept in place.
  3728→In case the O-Ring needs to be replaced, an O-Ring replacement kit or a set of O-Rings can
  3729→be purchased. For purchasing, contact your vendor where the Screwdriver has been
  3730→purchased.
  3731→To replace the O-Ring, follow the instructions below.
  3732→1.
  3733→Move the shank to the highest possible
  3734→value by using the user interface in the
  3735→robot or in the Web Client.
  3736→2. Detach the Screwdriver from the Quick
  3737→Changer.
  3738→3. Remove the lid.
  3739→4. If needed, break or remove the O-Ring by
  3740→pushing it down with the tool until it is out
  3741→of place. Then pull the O-Ring out.
  3742→5. Place the new O-Ring in the placement
  3743→tool.
  3744→6. Insert the placement tool at the end of the
  3745→shank.
  3746→MAINTENANCE
  3747→114
  3748→

  3750→=== PAGE 115 ===
  3751→7.
  3752→Push the placement tool to place the O-
  3753→Ring in the shank's slot.
  3754→8. Attach a bit holder and gently shake it to
  3755→make sure it is not loose.
  3756→Calibration
  3757→The Screwdriver has been calibrated at the factory to comply with the specifications stated in
  3758→the technical sheet. A calibration report has been provided in the Screwdriver packaging.
  3759→If used according to recommended guidelines and conditions and maintained correctly, the
  3760→Screwdriver performs according to these specifications across its lifetime.
  3761→In case an updated calibration report is required, contact the vendor from where the
  3762→Screwdriver has been purchased.

  4176→9. Troubleshooting
  4177→9.1. Robot Has Not Obtained an IP Address
  4178→If the Compute Box has not assigned an IP address to the robot, do the following:
  4179→Assign a static IP address to the robot that matches your current IP settings on your Compute
  4180→Box. The default IP address of the Compute Box is 192.168.1.1.
  4181→NOTE:
  4182→Change the last number in the IP address (if using 255.255.255.0 subnet
  4183→mask) to avoid an IP conflict with the Compute Box.
  4184→Example
  4185→If the default fixed (192.168.1.1) IP address is used on the Compute Box, then use the following
  4186→values:
  4187→•
  4188→IP address: 192.168.1.2
  4189→•
  4190→Subnet Mask: 255.255.255.0
  4191→9.2. Changing the DIP Switch Does Not Take Effect
  4192→To change the DIP switch network settings, first change the DIP switches and then cycle the
  4193→Compute Box/Eye Box power so the changes will take effect.
  4194→If the changes still do not take effect, wait one minute and then cycle the Compute Box/Eye
  4195→Box power once again.
  4196→9.3. Screwdriver Error Codes
  4197→9.3.1. Z-axis (Or General Init) Busy
  4198→Error code (on the Web Client and in the robot scripts): 2
  4199→Description: Z-axis (or general init) is busy.
  4200→Suggestion(s) to solve the error:
  4201→•
  4202→Power cycle the tool.
  4203→•
  4204→Ensure that the Compute Box software and the tool firmware are updated.
  4205→•
  4206→If the error is still present, return the tool to OnRobot for repair. Contact your local
  4207→distributor for more information.

# OnRobot Screwdriver for Techman Robots - 핵심 정보 정리 (v6.5.0)

**원본 파일**: `C:\Users\Administrator\Desktop\DAEHYUN_PRIVATE\TM Robot\User_Manual_For_Techman_Robots_Quick_Changer_Screwdriver_v6.5.0_EN.pdf`

## 1. Quick Changer 설정

### 타입 요구사항
- Screwdriver는 반드시 **Quick Changer Robot Side 4.5A** 버전을 사용해야 함
- Compute Box HW 버전 최소 **v3.4** 필요
- Compute Box SW 버전: **v6.5.x**

### Quick Changer 제품 라인업

| 이름 | Item # | 버전 |
|------|--------|------|
| QC-R I/O support | 102326 | QC-R I/O |
| QC-R v2 (단종) | 102037 | QC-R v2 |
| **QC-R v2-4.5A (단종)** | **104277** | **QC-R v2-4.5** |
| QC-R v3 | 109498 | QC-R v3 |
| Dual QC v2 (단종) | 101788 | Dual QC v2 |
| Dual QC v2-4.5A (단종) | 104293 | Dual QC v2-4.5 |
| Dual QC v3 | 109878 | Dual QC v3 |

### Quick Changer 기술 데이터

| 항목 | Quick Changer | Dual Quick Changer |
|------|--------------|-------------------|
| Rated payload | 25 kg | 30 kg |
| Permissible force | 400 N | 600 N |
| Permissible torque | 40 Nm | 40 Nm |
| Repeatability | +/-0.02 mm | +/-0.02 mm |
| IP Classification | IP67 | IP67 |
| Operating life | 5,000 cycles | 5,000 cycles |
| Operating temperature | 0~50 C | 0~50 C |

### 장착 방법

1. 툴을 Quick Changer 가까이 이동 (hook mechanism 정렬)
2. 툴을 뒤집어서 완전히 결합 - 클릭 소리가 나야 함
3. 분리 시: Quick Changer의 알루미늄 버튼을 누르고 역순으로 분리

---

## 2. Screwdriver TMflow 컴포넌트 노드 목록 및 파라미터

### 2.1 ScrewDriver_OR_SD_110_TIGHTEN

**기능**: 집어올린 나사를 조임. 샤프트를 내리면서 비트를 시계방향 회전. Z force 감지 시 동작 시작, 나사 길이만큼 이동 후 토크 초과 시 완료. 완료 후 10mm 후퇴.

| 파라미터 | 타입 | 범위 | 기본값 | 설명 |
|---------|------|------|--------|------|
| ZForceN | float | 18.0 ~ 30.0 N | 18 N | 샤프트에 가하는 힘 |
| ScrewLengthMM | float | 0.1 ~ 35.0 mm | 5 mm | 나사 길이 (삽입 거리) |
| TorqueN | float | 0.15 ~ 5.0 Nm | 0.5 Nm | 나사에 필요한 토크 |

### 2.2 ScrewDriver_OR_SD_110_LOOSEN

**기능**: 장착된 나사를 풂. 샤프트를 내리면서 반시계방향 회전. Z force 감지 시 동작 시작, 나사 길이만큼 이동 후 정지. 완료 후 10mm 후퇴.

| 파라미터 | 타입 | 범위 | 기본값 | 설명 |
|---------|------|------|--------|------|
| ZForceN | float | 18.0 ~ 30.0 N | 18 N | 샤프트에 가하는 힘 |
| ScrewLengthMM | float | 0.1 ~ 35.0 mm | 5 mm | 나사 길이 (추출 거리) |

### 2.3 ScrewDriver_OR_SD_110_PICKUP

**기능**: 스크류 프리젠터에서 나사를 집어올림. 샤프트를 내리면서 비트를 천천히 회전하여 나사를 픽업. Z force 감지 시 나사 길이 + 10mm만큼 후퇴.

| 파라미터 | 타입 | 범위 | 기본값 | 설명 |
|---------|------|------|--------|------|
| ZForceN | float | 18.0 ~ 30.0 N | 18 N | 샤프트에 가하는 힘 |
| ScrewLengthMM | float | 0.1 ~ 35.0 mm | 5 mm | 나사 길이 (후퇴량 결정) |

### 2.4 ScrewDriver_OR_SD_110_MOVE

**기능**: 샤프트를 특정 위치로 이동. 에러/safety 상태 복구에도 사용.

| 파라미터 | 타입 | 범위 | 기본값 | 설명 |
|---------|------|------|--------|------|
| ZPositionMM | float | 0 ~ 55 mm (비트 익스텐더 없을 때) | - | 0=완전후퇴, 55=완전전개. 비트 익스텐더 사용 시 GetParameters의 minShank/maxShank 참조 |

비트 익스텐더별 범위:
- None: 0 ~ 55 mm
- 50 mm 익스텐더: 50 ~ 105 mm
- 100 mm 익스텐더: 100 ~ 155 mm

### 2.5 ScrewDriver_OR_SD_110_SELFTAPPING

**기능**: 셀프태핑 나사를 사전 가공된 구멍에 체결. 85%의 최종 토크와 최대 3 Nm으로 태핑 단계 실행 후, 설정된 토크로 최종 체결.

| 파라미터 | 타입 | 범위 | 기본값 | 설명 |
|---------|------|------|--------|------|
| ZForceN | float | 18.0 ~ 30.0 N | 18 N | 샤프트에 가하는 힘 |
| ScrewLengthMM | float | 0.1 ~ 35.0 mm | 5 mm | 나사 길이. 포인팅 나사는 뾰족한 부분 제외하고 측정 |
| TargetTorqueN | float | 0.15 ~ 5.0 Nm | 0.5 Nm | 최종 목표 토크 |

### 2.6 ScrewDriver_OR_SD_110_PREMOUNT

**기능**: 나사를 주어진 길이까지 삽입하되 최종 체결(토크 적용)하지 않음. 머신 스크류 전용 (셀프태핑 불가).

| 파라미터 | 타입 | 범위 | 기본값 | 설명 |
|---------|------|------|--------|------|
| ZForceN | float | 18.0 ~ 30.0 N | 18 N | 샤프트에 가하는 힘 |
| ScrewLengthMM | float | 0.1 ~ 35.0 mm | 5 mm | 나사 길이 |
| TorqueLimitN | float | 0.15 ~ 5.0 Nm | 0.5 Nm | 프리마운트 중 최대 허용 토크 |

### 2.7 ScrewDriver_OR_SD_110_CONFIG

**기능**: 스크루드라이버에 설정 파라미터 전송.

| 파라미터 | 타입 | 범위 | 기본값 | 설명 |
|---------|------|------|--------|------|
| ExtenderLength | float | - | 0.0 mm | 비트 익스텐더 길이 [mm] |

**Gateway**: Success / Error (범위 초과 또는 미연결)

### 2.8 ScrewDriver_OR_SD_110_GetParameters

**기능**: 스크루드라이버의 현재 상태/파라미터를 읽어옴.

**출력 변수 목록**:

| 출력 변수 | 타입 | 설명 |
|-----------|------|------|
| statusZAxisBusy | int | 1=Z축 동작중, 0=아님 |
| additionalResults | int | 추가 상태 레지스터 값 (에러코드 0~10) |
| torqueNm | float | 현재 토크 [Nm] |
| ZForceN | float | Z축 현재 힘 [N] |
| statusScrewdriverBusy | int | 1=스크루드라이버 동작중, 0=아님 |
| zPositionMm | float | Z축 샤프트 위치 [mm] |
| torqueAngleGr | float | 마지막 작업의 토크 각도 기울기 [Nm/rad] |
| initializeZStallCurrentNotReached | int | 1=에러, 0=정상 |
| initializeNoZIndexMarkFound | int | 1=에러, 0=정상 |
| initializeUnableToHomeZAxis | int | 1=에러, 0=정상 |
| initializeZIndexPlacementNotOk | int | 1=에러, 0=정상 |
| initializeNoIndexMarkFoundOnTorqueEncoders | int | 1=에러, 0=정상 |
| initializeTooBigTorqueDifferenceDuringInitialization | int | 1=에러, 0=정상 |
| errorQCType | int | 1=잘못된 QC 타입, 0=정상 |
| errorPowerSupply | int | 1=잘못된 전원공급, 0=정상 |
| errorZAxisSafetyActivated | int | 1=Z축 안전 발동, 0=정상 |
| torqueAchievedNm | float | 마지막 명령의 달성 토크 [Nm] |
| minShank | float | 최소 샤프트 위치 [mm] |
| maxShank | float | 최대 샤프트 위치 [mm] |
| extenderLength | float | 익스텐더 길이 [mm] |

### 2.9 CB_OR_CB_GETIO (Compute Box I/O)

**기능**: Compute Box의 디지털 입력 핀 8개를 읽어서 전역 변수에 저장.

**출력**: `g_CB_OR_CB_IO_VALUES` (Bool 배열, 8개 요소 - 각 디지털 입력의 ON/OFF 상태)

---

## 3. 노드별 공통 Gateway (분기 출력)

모든 동작 노드(TIGHTEN, LOOSEN, PICKUP, MOVE, SELFTAPPING, PREMOUNT)의 Gateway:

| Gateway | 설명 |
|---------|------|
| **Success** | 작업 성공 |
| **InitializationError** | 초기화 실패 - `g_ScrewDriver_OR_SD_InitializationError` 확인 |
| **PowerError** | 전원 요건 미충족 - `g_ScrewDriver_OR_SD_PowerError` 확인 |
| **RuntimeError** | 작업 실패 - `g_ScrewDriver_OR_SD_RuntimeError` 확인 |
| **Safety** | 샤프트 최대 안전 힘 초과 - 안전 정지 발동 |
| **Timeout** | 시간 내 작업 미완료 - 에러코드 무효 |

**Safety/Runtime 에러 복구 방법**: SD_MOVE 노드로 샤프트를 새 위치로 이동시켜야 함.

| 2 | 나사가 체결되지 않음 (나사 없음 / 위치 오류) |
| 3 | 정확한 토크 대기 타임아웃 (4초) |
| 4 | 토크가 예기치 않게 초과 (조기 초과) |
| 5 | 나사 풀기 불가 (최대 토크 초과) |
| 6 | Z축이 끝에 도달 (스크루드라이버 팁을 나사에 더 가까이) |
| 7 | 이동 중 Z축 장애물 감지 |
| 8 | 토크 또는 전류 한계 초과 |
| 9 | Z축 과부하 |
| 10 | 토크가 예기치 않게 감소 (나사 파손 가능) |

## 7. Compute Box 설정

### 네트워크 설정

- **기본 IP**: 192.168.1.1
- **DIP Switch 설정**: DIP 3 = ON, DIP 4 = OFF (팩토리 기본값)
- **네트워크 모드**: Static IP / Dynamic IP / Default Static IP 선택 가능
- **로봇 IP 설정 예시**: 192.168.1.2 / Subnet 255.255.255.0 (마지막 옥텟만 변경하여 충돌 방지)
- DIP Switch 변경 후 반드시 전원 사이클 필요

### Web Client 접속

- 브라우저에서 Compute Box IP 입력
- 기본 계정: `admin` / `OnRobot` (최초 로그인 시 비밀번호 변경 필수, 8자 이상)
- 사용자 레벨: Administrator / Operator / User

| ERROR_SD_STATUS_32 | 32 | Init: 샤프트 인덱스 마크 미발견 | 전원 사이클 |
| ERROR_SD_STATUS_48 | 48 | Init: Z축 홈 이동 불가 / 과전류 | 전원 사이클 |
| ERROR_SD_STATUS_64 | 64 | Init: Z 인덱스 배치 이상 | 전원 사이클 |
| ERROR_SD_STATUS_80 | 80 | Init: 토크 인덱스 마크 미발견 | 전원 사이클 |
| ERROR_SD_STATUS_96 | 96 | Init: 초기화 중 토크 차이 과대 | 벨로우가 Screw-bit에 닿는지 확인, 윤활유 도포 |
| ERROR_SD_STATUS_112 | 112 | Init: 인덱스 마크 값 변경됨 | 전원 사이클 |

### Runtime 추가 코드 상세 해결방법

| 코드 | 설명 | 해결방법 |
|------|------|---------|
| 0 | 정상 | 조치 불필요 |
| 1 | 알 수 없는 명령 | 전원 사이클, SW/FW 업데이트 |
| 2 | 나사가 체결되지 않음 | 나사 유무 확인, 구멍/나사산 위치 확인 |
| 3 | 토크 대기 타임아웃 (4초) | 구멍 위치 확인, Screwing length 값 줄이기 |
| 4 | 토크 조기 초과 | Screwing length 값 늘리기, 재질 낮추기(셀프태핑) |
| 5 | 나사 풀기 불가 (최대 토크 초과) | 재시도. 5 Nm 이상으로 체결된 나사는 풀기 불가 |
| 6 | Z축 끝 도달 | 스크루드라이버 팁을 나사/구멍에 더 가까이 배치 |
| 7 | 이동 중 Z축 장애물 | 샤프트 끝이 충돌하지 않았는지 확인, 내부 이물질 확인 |
| 9 | Z축 과부하 | 전원 사이클, SW/FW 업데이트 |

## 9. 듀얼 스크루드라이버(D2SD) 관련 정보

이 매뉴얼(v6.5.0)에는 **D2SD(듀얼 스크루드라이버) 관련 내용이 포함되어 있지 않음**. Dual Quick Changer에 대한 스펙(기계적 사양, 페이로드)만 있으며, 스크루드라이버 2대 동시 사용(D2SD) 구성에 대한 별도 문서가 필요함.

참고로 컴포넌트명에 `D1_`/`D2_` 접두사가 붙은 컴포넌트는 Dual Quick Changer의 1차(primary)/2차(secondary) 측에 연결된 장치용이라는 언급만 있음.

## 10. 추가 실무 정보

### 스크루드라이버 동작 조건
- 스크루드라이버 바닥면과 작업면 거리: **0 ~ 8 mm** 이내여야 정상 동작
- 8mm 초과 시 에러 발생
- 하방향 또는 최대 측면(90도)까지만 작동. **상향 작동 시 보호 기능 발동**

### 안전 보호 기능
- 작동 중 40 N 이상의 힘 감지 시 자동으로 샤프트 후퇴
- 미작동 중 20 N 이상의 예기치 않은 힘 감지 시 일시적 샤프트 후퇴 (프로그램 중단 없음)
- 나사 길이 35mm 이하, 비트 익스텐더 미사용 시 완전한 안전 보호 기능 보장

### LED 상태 표시

| 색상 | 상태 |
|------|------|
| 꺼짐 | 전원 없음 |
| 녹색 (점등) | 대기/준비 완료 |
| 녹색 (점멸) | 초기화 중 |
| 주황색 (점등) | 동작 중 (샤프트 이동/회전) |
| 주황색 (점멸) | 작동 오류 |
| 빨간색 (점등) | 하드웨어 문제 |
| 빨간색 (점멸) | 안전 - 비상 정지 |

### TMflow SW 설치 순서 (컴포넌트 방식)
1. USB에 TM_Export 폴더 복사, USB 이름을 "TMROBOT"으로 변경
2. Settings > System > Import/Export > Import에서 OnRobot 폴더 선택
3. 관련 컴포넌트 선택 후 Import
4. Settings > Configuration > Component에서 컴포넌트 활성화
5. Network 설정에서 로봇 IP 확인 (기본 사용 권장)
6. Modbus TCP 설정에서 Compute Box IP 확인 (기본 192.168.1.1)
